source_metadata.title_raw: Steve Mitra - A-19-167 - August 19, 2019 - San Jose

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 19, 2019

Steve Mitra
Assistant County Counsel
County of Santa Clara
70 West Hedding St.
East Wing, 9th Floor
San Jose, CA 95110-1770

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-19-167

Dear Mr. Mitra:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("the Act") and Government Code Section 1090, et seq.¹ Please note that we are only providing advice under the Political Reform Act and Section 1090, not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest, including Public Contract Code.

Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

We are required to forward your request regarding Section 1090 and all pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the Santa Clara County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Under the Political Reform Act and Section 1090, may the Chief Operating Officer of the County of Santa Clara ("County") take part in the contract process between the County and non-profit organization Destination: Home SV ("Destination: Home"), given that the Chief Operating Officer also serves as a non-compensated member of the Destination: Home Governing Board?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-19-167
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

Yes. Under the Act, as a non-compensated officer of a non-profit organization, no
potentially disqualifying economic interest is implicated by Mr. Márquez's volunteer work. With
respect to Section 1090, as long as no other economic interest is implicated by a contract between
the County and Destination: Home, Mr. Márquez's non-compensated work does not constitute a
prohibited "financial interest" in any potential contract. However, he must ensure that his interest in
Destination: Home is noted in the County's official records.

FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER

Miguel Márquez is the Chief Operating Officer ("COO") of the County of Santa Clara
("County").

In June 2016, the County Board of Supervisors unanimously approved placing an affordable
housing bond approval question on the November 2016 ballot. At that election, Santa Clara County
voters approved the issuance of general obligation bonds in the amount of $950 million ("Housing
Bond") to create new affordable rental and homeowner housing opportunities, including for
homeless individuals.

Under the direction of Mr. Márquez, the Office of Supportive Housing is the County's lead
agency for implementing the Housing Bond, coordinating strategies to achieve housing production
targets, and maintaining partnerships that produce outcomes consistent with the measures of
success. The Office of Supportive Housing works with non-profit organization Destination: Home
and other public and private entities in the County to reduce and prevent homelessness, which
includes increasing the supply of housing and supportive housing to extremely low income and
special needs households.

Destination: Home is a public-private partnership that promotes collective impact strategies
to end homelessness in Santa Clara County. Destination: Home is organized as a nonprofit
501(c)(3) tax-exempt organization. The Articles of Incorporation for the organization state that
"[t]he specific and primary purpose of this corporation to end homelessness in Santa Clara County,
California also includes the following related purposes" of (1) "[s]upporting the goals of the Santa
Clara Community Plan to End Homelessness and the intent to make homelessness rare, brief and
non-recurring in Santa Clara County by providing additional funds necessary to support the
development and operation of permanent supportive and rapid re-housing units in Santa Clara
County;" and (2) "[s]trategically partnering with local public and private partners to [] share in the
joint approach of preventing and reducing homelessness for the Santa Clara County community's
poorest and most vulnerable residents."

Destination: Home has, among other things, developed a County-wide community plan to
end homelessness through a year-long process in 2014 that brought together over 200 stakeholders.
The resulting 2015-2020 Community Plan to End Homelessness is now the roadmap for how the
County will end homelessness, and it has been endorsed by the County of Santa Clara Board of
Supervisors. Destination: Home also supports the County's efforts to implement the Housing Bond,
including helping developers acquire sites for supportive housing through loans and grants, raising
awareness among residents in order to build a larger "pro-supportive housing" coalition within the

File No. A-19-167
Page No. 3

County, and building partnerships between government agencies, community-based organizations,
and the private sector.

In addition to being the COO of Santa Clara County, Mr. Márquez is also a member of the
Governing Board of Destination: Home. Other members of the Board include representatives of the
City of San Jose and the Santa Clara County Housing Authority. Except for the Executive Director,
members of the Governing Board are not provided with financial compensation (such as salary, per
diem, or reimbursement).

The County currently contracts with Destination: Home for services. Currently, the County
has a $6,811,334 contract with Destination: Home as part of a new pilot homelessness prevention
system in Santa Clara County and to coordinate implementation between the lead service provided
(Sacred Heart Community Services) and the program funders (such as the County, the City of San
Jose, and Google). Destination: Home also recently approved a grant to the County in the amount of
$489,000 to fund a County employee position for up to three years. The County will likely have an
ongoing relationship with Destination: Home, including making policies that affect the organization
and on future contracts with the organization.

ANALYSIS

The Act

Section 87100 of the Act provides, "[n]o public official at any level of state or local
government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial
interest." A public official has a financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100
if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable
from its effect on the public generally, on the official, a member of his or her immediate family, or
on certain enumerated economic interests. (Section 87103.) Among these enumerated interests is
"[a]ny source of income … aggregating five hundred dollars ($ 500) or more in value provided or
promised to, received by, the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made." "Income", however, does not include "travel expenses and per diem received from a bona
fide nonprofit entity exempt from taxation under Section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code."
(Section 82030(b)(2).)

Given that Mr. Márquez is not compensated for his work as a member of the Destination:
Home Governing Board, Destination: Home is not a potentially disqualifying "source of income"
economic interest under the Act. To the extent he has no other economic interests in the decision,
the Act's conflict of interest provisions would not prohibit Mr. Márquez from taking part in a
decision implicating Destination: Home.

Section 1090

Section 1090 provides, "[m]embers of the Legislature, state, county, district, judicial district,
and city officers or employees shall not be financially interested in any contract made by them in
their official capacity, or by any body or board of which they are members." However, Section
1091.5 provides that an officer or employee "shall not be deemed interested in a contract if his or

File No. A-19-167
Page No. 4

her interest is … [t]hat of a noncompensated officer of a non-profit, tax-exempt corporation, which,
as one of its primary purposes, supports the functions of the body or board or to which the body or
board has a legal obligation to give particular consideration, and provided further that this interest is
noted in its official records." (Section 1091.5(a)(8).) For the purposes of Section 1091.5(a)(8), "an
officer is 'noncompensated' even though he or she receives reimbursement from the nonprofit, tax-
exempt corporation for necessary travel and other actual expenses incurred in performing the duties
of his or her office."

As noted, Destination: Home is a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization. Mr. Márquez is an
officer of Destination: Home and not compensated for his work. Destination: Home's objective is to
end homelessness in Santa Clara County. The County also has an interest in achieving that
objective, as evidenced by the County Board of Supervisors' endorsement of the 2015-2020
Community Plan to End Homelessness. Accordingly, Destination: Home's primary purpose
supports the functions of the County and Mr. Márquez has a "noninterest" in Destination: Home as
an uncompensated officer of the organization. As long as his interest as a non-compensated officer
of Destination: Home is noted in the County's official records, and he does not have any other
economic interests implicated, Section 1090 does not prohibit him from taking part in contracts
between the County and Destination: Home.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Dave Bainbridge
General Counsel

By: Kevin Cornwall
Counsel, Legal Division

KMC:aja
